response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable individual-contributor engineer with a broad polyglot background and legitimate distributed-systems experience, but he falls critically short of the CTO requirements on virtually every leadership and domain-depth dimension. He has held no people-management or executive roles at any point in a 15-year career, has no track record scaling engineering organizations, and his AI/agent experience amounts to roughly six months of IC-level MCP tooling work at re:cinq — not the production multi-agent systems operating in regulated commercial environments the role demands. Fintech familiarity is superficial (one developer-level engagement at RIDE Capital), with no evidence of credit systems, compliance infrastructure, regulatory navigation, or board-level technical communication.
strengths: Broad polyglot technical foundation spanning distributed systems, real-time high-throughput services (millions of req/s at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby), and full-stack development across multiple paradigms
Direct hands-on exposure to agentic frameworks and MCP tooling at re:cinq, indicating genuine interest and early fluency in the relevant technical direction
Strong software-craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pairing) consistently applied across multiple roles
concerns: No people-management or engineering leadership experience at any level across a 15-year career — has never built, led, or scaled a team
AI and agentic-systems experience is shallow and very recent: six months as an IC contributor on MCP tooling, with no evidence of production multi-agent systems at commercial scale
No fintech infrastructure depth (credit risk, payment flows, embedded finance, compliance), no regulatory experience, and no demonstrated executive or board-level communication capability
